title: Birdcage Walk
content:
When the wind blows her veil
        And uncovers her laughter
    I cease, I turn pale.
    When the wind blows her veil
    From the woes I bewail
        Of love and hereafter:
    When the wind blows her veil
    I cease, I turn pale.
meaning:
D. H. Lawrence (David Herbert Richards)'s contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Birdcage Walk"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
